stories.title: Brazil's Congress reduces ex-President Bolsonaro's coup prison sentence
bullets: Hong Kong residents are traveling to Shenzhen for Labour Day, seeking better dining and attractions as the holiday begins.

stories.title: Madonna and Sabrina Carpenter share revitalising collab single ‘Bring Your Love’
bullets: Brazil's Congress has reduced former President Jair Bolsonaro's prison sentence for coup plotting from 27 years, defying President Lula da Silva's veto.

This decision could lead to further political instability and debates over accountability for actions taken during Bolsonaro's presidency.

stories.title: MTG outs MAGA rep who secretly ‘hates’ the president
bullets: Former congresswoman Marjorie Taylor Greene accused an unnamed Republican of secretly opposing Donald Trump during his 2024 nomination campaign.

stories.title: Two dead, including one Japanese, after lightplane crash in South Australia
bullets: A lightplane crashed into a hangar at Parafield Airport in South Australia on May 1, 2026, killing a Japanese learner pilot and an Australian instructor.

This tragedy highlights ongoing safety concerns in pilot training programs and may prompt regulatory reviews of flight operations.

stories.title: Mumbai-Delhi National Highway Gets India's First Barrier-Free Toll Plaza - Details
bullets: India inaugurated its first barrier-free toll plaza on the Mumbai-Delhi National Highway on May 1, 2026, enhancing toll collection efficiency.
The plaza utilizes Automatic Number Plate Recognition (ANPR) technology and FASTag integration for seamless toll payments.
This innovation is expected to reduce congestion and improve travel times, setting a precedent for future toll infrastructure in India.

stories.title: Why Dipika Kakar Is Drinking Ground Black Coffee, Husband Shoaib Ibrahim Explains

This lifestyle change reflects a growing trend towards healthier dietary choices among celebrities in India.

stories.title: Every song on the ‘Hokum’ soundtrack

stories.title: Ex-Spandau Ballet singer Ross Davidson sentenced to 14 years for rape and sexual assault
bullets: Ross Davidson, former lead singer of Spandau Ballet, was sentenced to 14 years in prison for multiple rapes and sexual assaults on May 1, 2026.
The court found Davidson guilty of crimes committed over several years, causing significant harm to his victims.

stories.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook warns of extended memory crunch. 'We'll look at a range of options'
bullets: In Apple's quarterly earnings report on May 1, 2026, CEO Tim Cook warned of ongoing memory constraints affecting production.
